channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chilean national arrested in Texas with jamming device that disabled officers' communications
item.description: A Chilean national is facing charges in Texas for burglary and interfering with arresting officers' communications by using a radio jamming device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The reality of a nuclear EMP attack: Why the US needs to be prepared
item.description: A nuclear electromagnetic pulse (EMP) strike could cripple the U.S. electrical grid and other critical infrastructure for months, an expert warned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China says US has 'gravely backpedaled' on Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's Foreign Ministry took issue Monday with a revised U.S. government fact sheet that removed a line on American opposition to independence for Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida sheriff says ICE partnership only the beginning in illegal migrant crackdown
item.description: St. Johns, Florida, Sheriff Robert Hardwick shared how law enforcement is working with federal immigration authorities to apprehend and enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 500 days of the Israel-Hamas war, by the numbers
item.description: Monday is the 500th day of the war triggered by Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023 attack into southern Israel. A tenuous ceasefire in the Gaza Strip has held for nearly a month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US special forces landing in Mexico to train marine infantry
item.description: The Americans will come fully armed as part of a mission to train the Mexican Navy’s Infantería de Marina (marine infantry) on conventional and non-conventional types of combat.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Sagui Dekel Chen is home after released by Hamas: Marco Rubio
item.description: American Sagui Dekel Chen was among the three men released in Khan Younis on Saturday in the latest victory for the fragile ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: Reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: 3 dead in fiery Wyoming tunnel crash
item.description: Another person has been confirmed dead as the investigation continues into a major, multi-vehicle crash in the westbound tunnel of I-80 in Green River, Wyoming, on Friday, according to the Wyoming Dept. of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, Fox 5

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration cuts reach FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: The Trump administration’s effort to slash the size of the federal workforce reached the Food and Drug Administration this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine says US presented doc to access minerals, offered almost nothing in return
item.description: “I didn’t let the ministers sign a relevant agreement because in my view it is not ready to protect us, our interest,” Zelenskyy told The Associated Press on Saturday in Munich.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: “Our adversaries are upping their game every day,” said former Department of Homeland Security cyber chief Suzanne Spaulding. “I’m worried that we are, at the same time, tearing down our defenses.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Inmate says Delphi prosecutor ignored his letters about Ron Logan confession in letter to FOX59
item.description: The inmate who is at the center of the latest legal battle in the Delphi double murders case said the prosecutor ignored repeated efforts to inform him about a supposed confession to the murders made by a man who was once a prime suspect.
item.dc:creator: Max Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: Does your kid have 'hurried child syndrome'? How to tell and what to do
item.description: The syndrome doesn't come from you rushing your kid to get dressed, but a different set of expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: President Donald Trump's suggestion about “getting rid of” the nation's disaster relief agency doesn't sound like a bad idea for some residents of western North Carolina who are still navigating the long recovery process from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NC sheriff says $59M from NYC migrants hotels should go to Helene survivors
item.description: On Monday, before $59 million in funds were officially taken back from New York City, Franklin County Sheriff Kevin White said the money should go to North Carolina residents.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming began sifting through the wreckage inside a highway tunnel Saturday after smoke, hazardous chemicals and structural concerns prevented them from determining how many people may have died in the fiery pileup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, KRON4

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats nervously wait on Trump response to court rulings
item.description: House Democrats howling over the flood of unilateral actions streaming from the White House are voicing confidence that the courts will provide a check on potential abuses of power. They’re less certain, however, about how President Trump will respond.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Eastern U.S. braces for flooding while fire-damaged California city now faces mudslides
item.description: SIERRA MADRE, Calif. (AP) — Much of the Eastern U.S. braced for a renewed round of harsh, soggy weather on Saturday, with thunderstorms and melting snow combining to pound the Ohio and Tennessee valleys. The lower Mississippi valley was facing the threat of strong tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, ABC 4

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Denny's to close dozens more restaurants in 2025
item.description: Denny's was already planning to close dozens of locations this year. Now it's planning to close even more.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: A framed image of President Donald Trump's mug shot from when he was indicted in Georgia in 2023 is hanging outside the Oval Office in the latest bit of redecorating since he moved back in.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Very cold weather is coming. US about to get 10th and chilliest polar vortex this winter
item.description: The coldest burst of Arctic air this season is coming to put an icy exclamation point on America's winter of repeated polar vortex invasions, meteorologists warn. And it will stay frozen there all next week.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Girlfriend calls 911 after seeing 3 kids dead on Facebook Live: Docs
item.description: Search warrants provided more insight into the devastating deaths of a mother and her three children in Chesapeake in an apparent murder-suicide on Feb. 7.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Order to drop New York mayor's case rocks Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Park Service erases the word 'transgender' from website commemorating Stonewall riot
item.description: References to transgender people were removed Thursday from a National Park Service website for the Stonewall National Monument, a park and visitor center in New York that commemorates a 1969 riot that became a pivotal moment for the LGBTQ+ rights movement.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois data shows inmates with violent records from shuttered prison sent to medium-security sites
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — Among the approximately 400 inmates transferred when Illinois' decrepit Stateville prison closed over the summer, 278 were convicted of murder and 100 more are serving time for other violent offenses.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As DOGE hammers away at the US government, Republicans stir with quiet objections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Katie Britt has been working to make sure the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency doesn't hit what she called “life-saving, groundbreaking research at high-achieving institutions,” including her state's beloved University of Alabama.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4
